title: nyssa biflora var. ursina
intro: Nyssa biflora var. ursina, also known as the eastern biltmore or eastern black tupelo, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of tannins and other compounds. The health benefits of Nyssa biflora var. ursina include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and topical creams for various medicinal purposes.
